title: अयप्पा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अयप्पा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
हरिहरसुतं देवं ध्यायेत् शास्तारमव्ययम्।
मणिकण्ठं महावीरं शबरीगिरिनायकम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
ॐ शास्ता शम्भुसुतः शान्तः शरणागतवत्सलः।
शबरीगिरिनिलयः शार्ङ्गपाणिसुतः शुभः॥ १॥

मणिकण्ठो महातेजा महादेवात्मजो बली।
महिषीमर्दनो मायी माधवात्मजमोहनः॥ २॥

धर्मशास्ता दयापूर्णो दानवान्तकरो दमी।
दाक्षायणीसुतो दिव्यो देवदेवो दिगम्बरः॥ ३॥

पम्पापतिः पद्महस्तः पापनाशनकारकः।
पन्थलेशः पवित्रात्मा पालनकर्ता परात्परः॥ ४॥

हरिहरात्मजो हव्यो हिरण्यगर्भसम्भवः।
हुताशनप्रभो हृद्यो हालास्यक्षेत्रपालकः॥ ५॥

ब्रह्मचारी बृहद्भानुर्भगवान् भक्तवत्सलः।
भवभयहरो भद्रो भूतेशो भवनाशनः॥ ६॥

वनवासी वरदात्मा विश्वरक्षाकरो विभुः।
वीरो विजयदो वन्द्यो वेदवेद्यो विशारदः॥ ७॥

योगी योगपरो ज्ञानी यज्ञभुक् यज्ञरक्षकः।
यमदण्डधरो युक्तो यशस्वी यतिवत्सलः॥ ८॥

नीलकण्ठसुतो नित्यो निरञ्जनो निरामयः।
नारायणात्मको नागराजवाहनसंस्थितः॥ ९॥

गजवाहनसम्पूज्यो गुरुमूर्तिर्गुणाश्रयः।
गिरिजानन्दनो गोप्ता गुहापूज्यो गणेश्वरः॥ १०॥

सर्वरोगहरो देवः सर्वपापविमोचनः।
सर्वसम्पत्प्रदो शान्तः सत्यधर्मपरायणः॥ ११॥

तत्त्वमस्यार्थबोधी च तपस्वी तारको महान्।
त्रिगुणातीतरूपश्च त्रैलोक्यपतिरव्ययः॥ १२॥

इरुमुडिप्रियो देवो एरुमेलिमहोत्सवः।
मकरज्योतिरूपश्च मण्डलव्रतपूजितः॥ १३॥

कलियुगवरदश्च करुणासागरो गुरुः।
कोटिसूर्यप्रभो कान्तः कल्मषघ्नो कृपानिधिः॥ १४॥

स्वामिशरणमन्त्रश्च शरणागतरक्षकः।
सर्वलोकहिते रतो अयप्पो देवनायकः॥ १५॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
सहस्रनामस्तोत्रं तु शास्तुर्यः पठतेऽनिशम्।
मण्डलव्रतसम्पन्नो मोक्षमाप्नोति शाश्वतम्॥

सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तः सर्वसिद्धिसमन्वितः।
अयप्पस्य प्रसादेन ब्रह्मलोकं स गच्छति॥

॥ इति श्री अयप्पा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ayyappa Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Hari-hara-sutam Devam Dhyayet Shastaram-avyayam.
Mani-kantham Maha-viram Shabari-giri-nayakam.

|| Stotram ||
Om Shasta Shambhu-sutah Shantah Sharanagata-vatsalah.
Shabari-giri-nilayah Sharnga-pani-sutah Shubhah. || 1 ||

Mani-kantho Maha-teja Mahadeva-atmajo Bali.
Mahishi-mardano Mayi Madhava-atmaja-mohanah. || 2 ||

Dharma-shasta Daya-purno Danava-antakaro Dami.
Dakshayani-suto Divyo Deva-devo Digambarah. || 3 ||

Pampa-patih Padma-hastah Papa-nashana-karakah.
Panthaleshash Pavitra-atma Palana-karta Paratparah. || 4 ||

Hari-hara-atmajo Havyo Hiranya-garbha-sambhavah.
Huta-ashana-prabho Hridyo Halasya-kshetra-palakah. || 5 ||

Brahmachari Brihad-bhanur-bhagavan Bhakta-vatsalah.
Bhava-bhaya-haro Bhadro Bhutesho Bhava-nashanah. || 6 ||

Vana-vasi Varada-atma Vishva-raksha-karo Vibhuh.
Viro Vijaya-do Vandyo Veda-vedyo Visharadah. || 7 ||

Yogi Yoga-paro Jnani Yajna-bhuk Yajna-rakshakah.
Yama-danda-dharo Yukto Yashasvi Yati-vatsalah. || 8 ||

Nila-kantha-suto Nityo Niranjano Niramayah.
Narayana-atmako Naga-raja-vahana-samsthitah. || 9 ||

Gaja-vahana-sampujyo Guru-murtir-guna-ashrayah.
Girija-nandano Gopta Guha-pujyo Ganeshwarah. || 10 ||

Sarva-roga-haro Devah Sarva-papa-vimochanah.
Sarva-sampat-prado Shantah Satya-dharma-parayanah. || 11 ||

Tattvam-asi-artha-bodhi Cha Tapasvi Tarako Mahan.
Tri-guna-atita-rupash-cha Trailokya-patir-avyayah. || 12 ||

Irumudi-priyo Devo Erumeli-mahotsavah.
Makara-jyoti-rupash-cha Mandala-vrata-pujitah. || 13 ||

Kali-yuga-varadash-cha Karuna-sagaro Guruh.
Koti-surya-prabho Kantah Kalma-sha-ghno Kripa-nidhih. || 14 ||

Svami-sharana-mantrash-cha Sharanagata-rakshakah.
Sarva-loka-hite Rato Ayyappo Deva-nayakah. || 15 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Sahasra-nama-stotram Tu Shastur-yah Pathate-nisham.
Mandala-vrata-sampanno Moksham-aapnoti Shashvatam.

Sarva-papa-vinirmuktah Sarva-siddhi-samanvitah.
Ayyappasya Prasadena Brahma-lokam Sa Gachchhati.

|| Iti Shri Ayyappa Sahasranama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हरिहरसुत, अव्यय, मणिकण्ठ, महावीर, शबरीगिरि नायक श्री अयप्पा शास्ता का ध्यान करें।

शास्ता, शम्भुसुत, शान्त, शरणागतवत्सल, शबरीगिरिनिलय, मणिकण्ठ, महातेजस्वी, महादेव के पुत्र, बली, महिषीमर्दन, माधव के पुत्र, धर्मशास्ता, दयापूर्ण, दानवान्तकर, पम्पापति, पापनाशक, हरिहरात्मज, ब्रह्मचारी, भक्तवत्सल, भवभयहर, वनवासी, विश्वरक्षक, योगी, तत्त्वमस्यार्थबोधी, इरुमुडिप्रिय, मकरज्योतिरूप, कलियुगवरद, शरणागतरक्षक - इन नामों से अयप्पा स्वामी की स्तुति की गई है।

जो मनुष्य निरन्तर इस सहस्रनाम स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है, मण्डल व्रत सम्पन्न कर शाश्वत मोक्ष प्राप्त करता है। सभी पापों से मुक्त, सर्वसिद्धि से सम्पन्न होकर अयप्पा स्वामी के प्रसाद से ब्रह्मलोक को जाता है।

viniyog.kilak: श्री अयप्पा प्रसाद सिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः
viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्री अयप्पा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रस्य नारद ऋषिः, अनुष्टुप् छन्दः, श्री धर्मशास्ता देवता, ॐ बीजम्, ह्रीं शक्तिः, श्री अयप्पा प्रसाद सिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः।

benefits: Invokes the thousand names and blessings of Lord Ayyappa
Invokes moksha (eternal liberation) to devoted reciters
Especially powerful during Mandala Kalam (41-day vrat period)
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and purifies the soul
Invokes the blessing of sarva-siddhi (all spiritual accomplishments)
Invokes the spiritual merit of sabarimala pilgrimage
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Ayyappa, Ayyappa Sahasranama Stotram (अयप्पा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्) stands as a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names. Attributed to Dharma Shastra Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to invoke the thousand names and blessings of Lord Ayyappa and grant the spiritual merit of Sabarimala pilgrimage. It is also said to grant moksha (eternal liberation) to devoted reciters. For best results, devotees chant this on Saturday (Shanivar). With 15 verses and a reading time of about 30 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Ayyappa Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ayyappa Sahasranama in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. 1000 names of Lord Ayyappa for devotion, moksha, and spiritual accomplishment.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Ayyappa and what does this sahasranama invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Ayyappa, also called Hariharaputra, Manikanta or Sastha, is the celestial son born from the union of Lord Vishnu (in his Mohini form) and Lord Shiva, embodying the synthesis of the Vaishnava and Shaiva traditions. He is one of the most beloved deities of South India, particularly Kerala and Tamil Nadu, and his principal shrine is at Sabarimala in the Pathanamthitta district of Kerala — visited by tens of millions of pilgrims annually. The Ayyappa Sahasranama invokes him by 1000 names spanning his identity as Hariharaputra (son of Hari and Hara), his Manikanta form (the bell-necked baby found by King Rajashekhara), his Sastha aspect as the lord of dharma, his Sabarimala manifestation, and his cosmic role as the dispeller of evil and the guide of true seekers. (Sources: Wikipedia Ayyappan; Hindupedia Sabarimala; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Ayyappa Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayyappa Sahasranama is preserved in classical tradition associated with the Bhutanatha (Sastha) Upapurana and the broader Dharma Shasta puranic and tantric corpus. The Pancharatra Agama and certain Tantra-Samhita compilations preserve Sastha sahasranama recensions which were systematised in Kerala under the influence of the Travancore royal patronage of Sabarimala. The version most widely circulated today is used in the daily liturgy at Sabarimala and at the Sastha temples of Kerala and Tamil Nadu — Aryankavu, Achankovil, Erumeli, Kulathupuzha and Pandalam. The hymn is widely circulated through Stotra Nidhi, Vedadhara, Sanskritdocuments.org and major regional pothi prints in Malayalam, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada and Hindi. (Sources: Wikipedia Ayyappan; Hindupedia Sastha; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Ayyappa Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Saturday is the principal weekday for Ayyappa worship in Kerala and Tamil Nadu tradition. The supreme annual occasion is the Mandala Pooja vratam — the 41-day austerity beginning around mid-November and culminating at Sabarimala on Mandala day in late December, during which devotees wear black or blue dhotis, observe celibacy and vegetarian diet, and recite the sahasranama and Ayyappa stotras daily. The Makaravilakku-Makara Jyothi festival on Makar Sankranti (January 14-15) is the climactic peak when millions converge on Sabarimala. Other peak occasions include Ayyappa Jayanti, Vishu (Kerala New Year), Onam, and the regular Sastha-preeti puja days. Devotees facing planetary affliction, fertility issues, or seeking moksha often recite the sahasranama daily. (Sources: Drikpanchang Mandala Pooja; Hindupedia Sabarimala; Wikipedia Makaravilakku.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sabarimala pilgrimage and its connection to the sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sabarimala pilgrimage is one of the largest annual religious gatherings in the world, drawing tens of millions of devotees to the Ayyappa shrine on the Sabari Hill of the Periyar Tiger Reserve in Kerala's Western Ghats. Devotees observe a 41-day vratam — wearing black or dark blue mundus, abstaining from meat, alcohol and sexual relations, walking barefoot, and addressing each other as Ayyappa Swami in recognition of the divine within. They carry the irumudi (a two-pouch cloth bundle of offerings) on their heads to climb the 18 sacred steps (pathinettam padi) to the sanctum. Throughout this period the Ayyappa Sahasranama, the Saranam Ayyappa chants, and the Bhuta Natha Ashtakam are recited daily as the principal scriptural anchors of the vratam. (Sources: Wikipedia Sabarimala; Hindupedia Mandala Vratam; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ayyappa Sahasranama phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources promise that one who recites the Ayyappa Sahasranama daily with bhakti gains comprehensive protection — material protection from enemies, disease, planetary affliction, accidents and untimely death; emotional protection from grief, addiction and depression; and spiritual protection from negative influences and obstacles to dharma. Specific rewards include success in righteous undertakings, fertility and the birth of virtuous children (Ayyappa is invoked as a deity of fertility blessing for childless couples), restoration of lost wealth and property, and the dispelling of irrational fears. Spiritual rewards include the strengthening of brahmacharya during the Mandala vratam, devotion to Hari-Hara, freedom from the bondage of sense-gratification, and ultimately moksha through the grace of the Lord. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia Sastha; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Hari, Hara and Ayyappa?
seoFaqs.answer: Ayyappa's identity as Hariharaputra — the son of Hari (Vishnu) and Hara (Shiva) — embodies the great theological synthesis of Vaishnavism and Shaivism that has flourished in Kerala and Tamil Nadu for centuries. According to the Bhagavata and Brahmanda Purana narratives, when Lord Vishnu assumed the Mohini form to distribute the amrita to the gods, Lord Shiva was so enchanted that from their union Ayyappa was born. He is therefore depicted with attributes of both — the bow and arrow of Vishnu's Rama avatar, the meditative posture of Shiva, the bell of Manikanta, and the chinmudra of cosmic teaching. His worship transcends sectarian boundaries, drawing both Vaishnava and Shaiva devotees, and at Sabarimala devotees of every caste and faith are welcome — the famous saying Tat Tvam Asi (You Are That) is inscribed at the entrance. (Sources: Hindupedia Hariharaputra; Wikipedia Sabarimala; Bhagavata Purana 8.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Ayyappa Sahasranama be recited by anyone, including women?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayyappa Sahasranama itself, as a stotra preserved in the open puranic and tantric tradition, is recitable by devotees of all genders, castes and ages — many women across Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Karnataka recite it in daily home puja and during Mandala period for the welfare of pilgrim family members. The traditional restrictions associated with Sabarimala temple entry have specifically related to physical pilgrimage to the Sabari Hill shrine and have been the subject of recent legal and cultural discussion; readers should consult current temple authority guidance for pilgrimage details. The recitation of the sahasranama, observance of devotional vrats, performance of home puja with Ayyappa images, and participation in Ayyappa bhajan groups are open to all devotees regardless of gender. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Drikpanchang.)
